assumptions: overflow: 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the subtraction operation.
The overflow flag is set to 1 if an overflow condition is detected; otherwise, it is set to 0.

expected_properties: Checks are limited to reset-safe, protocol-safe, or output-visible behavior.
No fixed latency or exact cycle count is assumed.

cases.goal: Confirm that the module produces a deterministic output for arbitrary stable inputs.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Enrichment adds explicit functional expectations while preserving the original conservative intent.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Only deterministic, contract-supported stimulus is used; otherwise the case is downgraded.
Provide deterministic, contract‑compliant stimulus to A and B.
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Use a reset‑safe observation window; do not rely on fixed latency.

notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.

expected_properties: result equals A - B for a wide range of random operand pairs
overflow flag follows sign‑overflow rules defined in the contract
cases.goal: Validate functional correctness of subtraction and overflow detection across a statistically significant set of random inputs.
notes: Uses deterministic stimulus generation (pseudo‑random seed) to keep the case repeatable.
stimulus_intent: Apply a series of random 64‑bit values to inputs A and B while keeping the design in a stable, reset‑safe state.
timing_assumptions: Observe result and overflow after inputs have been stable for at least one cycle; no fixed latency is assumed.

expected_properties: overflow is asserted when A is positive max and B is negative min leading to a negative result
overflow is asserted when A is negative min and B is positive max leading to a positive result
overflow is de‑asserted for all non‑overflow sign combinations
cases.goal: Exercise sign‑overflow boundary conditions to ensure overflow flag correctness at extreme operand values.
notes: Focuses on the two classic overflow scenarios described in the spec.
stimulus_intent: Drive A and B with extreme signed values (e.g., 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF, 0x8000000000000000) that trigger sign overflow.
timing_assumptions: Result and overflow are sampled after inputs have settled; no exact cycle count is required.

expected_properties: Each result and overflow corresponds to the immediately preceding A/B pair
No spurious toggling of outputs occurs between consecutive operations
cases.goal: Verify correct operation when multiple subtractions are issued back‑to‑back without idle cycles.
notes: Ensures the design can handle a stream of operand pairs.
stimulus_intent: Apply a rapid sequence of distinct A/B pairs, changing inputs every cycle.
timing_assumptions: Outputs are monitored continuously; latency is unknown but each output must eventually reflect its input pair.
